From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Trade union

National Amalgamated Union of Labour

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Merged into | [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers") |
| Founded | 1888 |
| Dissolved | 1924 |
| Headquarters | 4 Higham Place, [Newcastle upon Tyne](/wiki/Newcastle_upon_Tyne "Newcastle upon Tyne") |
| Location | * [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Key people | [J. N. Bell](/wiki/Joseph_Nicholas_Bell "Joseph Nicholas Bell"), General Secretary |
| Affiliations | [TUC](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress"), [NTWF](/wiki/National_Transport_Workers%27_Federation "National Transport Workers' Federation"), [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") |

The **National Amalgamated Union of Labour** (**NAUL**) was a [general union](/wiki/General_union "General union") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") was founded in Feb 1889 as the United Tyne and District Labourers Association and in March 1889 the Amalgamated Society of Shipyard Helpers and General Labourers of the River Wear merged with it. By 1890 the union was recruiting heavily in the London, Belfast and Barrow areas and absorbed the Sheffield and District Navvies and General Labourers Society and changed its name to the Tyneside and National Labour Union of Great Britain and Ireland. The Annual Delegate Meeting of 1892 voted to change its name to the National Amalgamated Union of Labour. By 1897, it claimed 22,397 members, making it the fourth-largest union in the UK. It affiliated to the [Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress") in 1912.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

While the union accepted all workers, most of its members were involved in shipbuilding, ship repair, and engineering. In North East England, it also represented the majority of dockers, and many iron and steel workers, while in South Yorkshire, it represented most surface workers at coal mines. In London, it represented many cement workers, and around the country it had a significant number of chemical workers, builders' labourers and tramway maintenance workers.[[3]](#cite_note-nugmw-3)

Following mergers with a large number of small trade unions, including the [London and Counties Labour League](/wiki/London_and_Counties_Labour_League "London and Counties Labour League"), by 1919, the union had 148,000 members spread across the UK and was led by [Joseph N. Bell](/wiki/Joseph_N._Bell "Joseph N. Bell"). He formed the [National Amalgamated Workers Union](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Workers_Union&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Amalgamated Workers Union (page does not exist)") as a loose confederation with the [Municipal Employees Association](/wiki/Municipal_Employees_Association "Municipal Employees Association") (MEA) and the [Workers Union](/wiki/Workers_Union "Workers Union"), but this dissolved in 1922, shortly before Bell's death.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1) Membership peaked at 170,000 in 1920, but then dropped rapidly, falling to only 53,000 by 1924. Women were initially not permitted to join the union, but this policy was changed, and by 1921 it had 25,000 women members, but this fell dramatically to only 2,000 in 1924.[[3]](#cite_note-nugmw-3)

In 1924, the union merged with the [National Union of General Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_Workers_(UK) "National Union of General Workers (UK)") and the MEA to form the [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers").[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1) Of the union's former delegates, Spence became Assistant General Secretary of the new union, while [Alfred James Bailey](/wiki/Alfred_James_Bailey "Alfred James Bailey"), Sam Bradley and [Tom Williamson](/wiki/Tom_Williamson,_Baron_Williamson "Tom Williamson, Baron Williamson") became district secretaries.[[3]](#cite_note-nugmw-3)

## Election results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Election results")]

From 1914 to 1922, the union sponsored several candidates in Parliamentary elections:

| Election | Party | Constituency | Candidate | Votes | % | Position[[4]](#cite_note-4) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [1914 by-election](/wiki/1914_Leith_Burghs_by-election "1914 Leith Burghs by-election") | [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | [Leith Burghs](/wiki/Leith_Burghs_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Leith Burghs (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Joseph Nicholas Bell](/wiki/Joseph_Nicholas_Bell "Joseph Nicholas Bell") | 3,346 | 24.5 | 3 |
| [1918 general election](/wiki/1918_UK_general_election "1918 UK general election") | [Independent Labour](/wiki/Independent_Labour "Independent Labour") | [Sheffield Central](/wiki/Sheffield_Central_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Sheffield Central (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Alfred James Bailey](/wiki/Alfred_James_Bailey "Alfred James Bailey") | 5,959 | 37.3 | 2[[5]](#cite_note-5) |
| [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | [Aberavon](/wiki/Aberavon_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Aberavon (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Robert Williams](/wiki/Robert_Williams_(trade_union_leader) "Robert Williams (trade union leader)") | 7,758 | 35.7 | 2 |
| [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | [Widnes](/wiki/Widnes_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Widnes (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Tom Williamson](/wiki/Tom_Williamson,_Baron_Williamson "Tom Williamson, Baron Williamson") | 7,821 | 40.4 | 2 |
| [1922 general election](/wiki/1922_UK_general_election "1922 UK general election") | [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") | [Newcastle upon Tyne East](/wiki/Newcastle_upon_Tyne_East_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Newcastle upon Tyne East (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Joseph Nicholas Bell](/wiki/Joseph_Nicholas_Bell "Joseph Nicholas Bell") | 10,084 | 43.1 | 1 |

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Leadership")]

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: General secretaries")]

:   1888: William Stanley
:   1891: Alfred T. Dipper
:   1898: [J. N. Bell](/wiki/Joseph_Nicholas_Bell "Joseph Nicholas Bell")
:   1923: [Ralph Spence](/wiki/Ralph_Spence_(trade_unionist) "Ralph Spence (trade unionist)")

### Corresponding secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Corresponding secretaries")]

:   1923: *Post vacant*
